content:
1. Character Decisions & Consequences (McKee’s Story Principles)

Robert McKee argues that a story moves forward only when a character makes a choice—and that every scene should involve a decision.

Example from Whiplash (Direct Screenplay Excerpts)

👉 Scene: Andrew’s first encounter with Fletcher in practice

FLETCHER: “Then why did you stop playing?”
ANDREW: “I thought—”
(then, blanching,)
ANDREW: “I’m sorry, I misun—”
FLETCHER: “I asked you why you stopped playing. Your version of an answer was to turn into a wind-up drummer monkey.”

• Here, Andrew has to make a decision: does he speak up and risk Fletcher’s wrath, or does he back down?
• Every moment in the film is about choice.

🔥 Key Writing Rule:
👉 In every scene, force your character to make a decision—no passive protagonists.

2. The Iceberg Principle: Reality, People, and Art
context: From document: Analyzing the Common Threads in Whiplash, Real, and Vagabond — chunk 5 (chunk 5/14)
